retry_reason: refcount_set is unconditional and always succeeds; failure can only occur from xa_alloc (ID reservation), not from the refcount operation.
contract_summary: This is a set-wrapper that creates a new iommufd_object with initial reference counts set to 1. It allocates memory, initializes the type and refcounts, and reserves an ID in the xarray (without publishing the pointer). The caller receives an object with an initial reference; the caller must complete initialization and call iommufd_object_finalize() to make the object visible. The caller is responsible for properly putting the object when done, using iommufd_object_unref() or similar. If xa_alloc fails, the function returns an error and frees the allocated memory.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate and initialize an iommufd_object, setting its refcounts (wait_cnt and users) to 1 via refcount_set. The function also performs xarray ID reservation, which is a necessary part of object creation but not purely refcount management.

check.reason: Both refcount_set calls operate on obj->wait_cnt and obj->users, where obj is the return value. The access paths start from the return variable (obj), trace through the struct fields (wait_cnt/users) to the refs field inside refcount_t, and the type chains are consistent with the callee information and code.
